title: Minibulk v2 (modified Prime-seq)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f6rjd","text":"Cost-efficient library generation by early barcoding has been central in propelling single-cell RNA sequencing. Here, we optimize and validate prime-seq, an early barcoding bulk RNA-seq method. We show that it performs equivalently to TruSeq, a standard bulk RNA-seq method, but is fourfold more cost-efficient due to almost 50-fold cheaper library costs. We conclude that prime-seq is currently one of the best options to set up an early barcoding bulk RNA-seq protocol from which many labs would profit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9sp5r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hug6","text":"WEHI advanced Genomics facility amendments from Prime-seq:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":58}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhi3c","text":"The plasticware has been changed from 96w plates to 384w and volumes have been reduced 1/4. The reverse transcription of Prime-Seq does not use RNase inhibitors, which I have included.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hvio","text":"The primers used for full length cDNA amplification have been taken from 10x Genomics chemistry. This adds compatibility with ONT sequencing (FLT-Seq) developed by Jafar and compatibility with 10x Genomics indexing primers set TT.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Clean all surfaces and pipettes with PCR clean wipes
Thaw frozen buffers and primers on ice
Prepare fresh 80% EtOH

Input to minibulk v2 is extracted RNA or sorted cells. It is essential, however, that the samples either have the same input or that they are normalized after the RNA is extracted, otherwise sequencing depth per sample will be impacted.
Normalize the samples so they are all the same concentration. Minimum : 100 pg Maximum : 4 ng
Transfer 1.2 µL of the normalized RNA to a new plate. Return the unnormalized RNA to the freezer.

Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix Store on ice until use A B C D 1 Reagent Well uL 384w plate + 20% Conc 2 UltraPure Water 0.5625 285.1 3 Maxima RT Buffer (5x) 0.5 253.4 1x 4 100mM dNTPs 0.0125 6.3 500nM 5 100uM Biotin RAP-TSO 0.025 12.7 1uM 6 SuperaseIN 0.125 63.4 1U 7 Maxima H Minus RT 0.075 38.0 1U 8 Total (incl RNA) 2.5 1267.2
Add 1.3 µL Reverse Transcription Mix to each well with the FlexDrop
Incubate for 01:00:00 at 50 °C
Pool all wells of each plate by inversion into a SBS reservoir using a centrifuge 400 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes collect into a 5mL LoBind tube.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads

Wash with 2 mL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant Volume of EtOH should be adjusted depending on the number of samples. More samples will require more EtOH to cover the beads completely.
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Daniel prefers 2 minutes to 5 minutes to reduce risk of overdrying. It is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute the beads in 45 µL of UltraPure Water
Prepare Pre Amplification Mix A B 1 Reagent 1x 2 KAPA HiFi 2x RM 25 µL 3 10uM RAP_PCR-1_Fwd 2 µL 4 10uM RAP-FL_Rev 2 uL 5 UltraPure Water 1 µL 6 Total 30 µL
Add 30 µL Pre Amplification Mix to 20 µL cDNA sample

Incubate the Pre Amplification PCR as follows: A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 98 C 3 min 1 cycle 3 Denaturation 98 C 15 sec 10 - 18 cycles* 4 Annealing 65 C 30 sec 5 Elongation 72 C 4 min 6 Final Elongation 72 C 10 min 1 cycle 7 Storage 4 C ∞ Adjust the number of cycles based on input (sample number, cell number, or concentration). As a general guide we recommend: Daniel's experience is it is better to err on more PCR cycles for this full length PCR and less for the 2nd library PCR. A B 1 Total RNA Input Cycles 2 1ng 18 3 10 ng 16 4 50 ng 14 5 100 ng 12 6 >500 ng 10
Mix sample with 40 µL SPRIselect for a 0.8x SPRI to sample ratio
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard supernatant
Wash with 180 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute cDNA in 12 µL UltraPure Water
Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer 10 µL to a new PCR tube or plate Stopping Point. cDNA samples can be safely stored at -20 °C and protocol can be continued at a later date.
Quantify the cDNA using Qubit DNA HS following the manufacturer's protocol. Use 2 μl of clean cDNA for quantification.

Quality check the cDNA using the Tapestation D5000 tape. Passing the cDNA quality check does not guarantee that the data will be of high quality, however, if the cDNA fails the quality check it will usually not yield good libraries and will therefore generate lower quality data. Example tapestation D5000 HS

The input to library preparation is amplified full length cDNA from each pooled 384w plate. Before starting, read the library preparation section carefully as there are a few steps that are very time sensitive.

Prepare Fragmentation Mix Take note of the total cDNA input in ng as it will be relevant in later PCR step A B 1 Reagent 1x 2 Ultra II FS Reaction Buffer 1.4 µL 3 Ultra II FS Enzyme Mix 0.4 µL 4 Amplified cDNA 2.5 µL 5 Water 1.7 µL 6 Total 6 µL Ensure that the Ultra II FS Reaction Buffer is completely thawed. If a precipitate is seen in the buffer, pipette up and down several times to break it up, and quickly vortex to mix. Place on ice until use. Vortex the Ultra II FS Enzyme Mix for 5-8 seconds prior to adding to master mix for optimal performance.
Vortex the Fragmentation Mix for 00:00:05 and incubate in thermocycler immediately

Incubate the Fragmentation reaction in a thermocycler as follows: A B C 1 Step Temperature Time 2 Pre-Cool 4 C ∞ 3 Fragmentation 37 C 5 min 4 A Tailing and Phosphorylation 65 C 30 min 5 Storage 4 C ∞ Set heated lid to 75° C. Make sure the lid is at the correct temperature before you start the reaction. After placing your samples, skip the 4C step to begin the 37C incubation once you have added your samples.

Prepare Adapter Ligation Mix A B 1 Reagent 1x 2 NEBNext Ultra II Ligation Master Mix 6 µL 3 NEBNext Ligation Enhancer 0.2 µL 4 1.5uM IDT custom adapter (pre-annealed tenX_top and tenX_bottom) 0.5 µL 5 Total 6.7 µL
Add 6.7 µL Adapter Ligation Mix to each fragmentation sample
Incubate for 00:15:00 at 20 °C in a thermocycler Set heated lid to off
Add 7.7 µL Buffer EB to Samples

Mix samples with 16 µL SPRI select beads for a 0.8x SPRI ratio The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and discard supernatant.
Wash with 180 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute samples in 22 µL water for 00:05:00 off magnet Incubate 00:02:00 on magent Transfer 20 µL sample to a new tube

Add 5 µL of Dual Index Kit TT Set A 10x Genomics Catalog #1000215 to each sample. Take care to change tips and avoid cross contamination Ensure a different index for each samples as this is the unique index that will be used for demultiplexing libraries.
Prepare Library PCR Mix by adding 25 µL Kappa HiFi polymerase to the 0.2mL tube containing purified adapter ligated sample.

Incubate the Library PCR reaction as follows: A B C D 1 Step Temperature Time Cycles 2 Initial Denaturation 98 C 30 sec 1 cycle 3 Denaturation 98 C 20 sec 8 cycles* 4 Annealing 54 C 30sec 5 Elongation 72 C 20 sec 6 Final Elongation 72 C 2 min 1 cycle 7 Storage 4 C ∞ Adjust the number of cycles based on cDNA input. As a general guide we recommend: A B 1 cDNA Input Cycles 2 20 ng 8 3 10 ng 9 4 5 ng 10 Daniel found the more PCR cycles used the more the library distribution skews to the left. Better to err on the fewer PCR cycles side.
Mix Index PCR with 25 µL, 0.5x ratio SPRI select beads
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and transfer 75 µL supernatant to clean well . Be careful NOT to discard! This is your library.

Mix supernatant with 15 µL, total 0.8x ratio SPRI select beads The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and discard supernatant.
Wash with 180 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet, discard the supernatant
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute in 22 µL UltraPure Water .

Incubate for 00:05:00 and then place on magnet until clear. Transfer 20 µL eluted library to new tube. Stopping point. The libraries can be safely stored at -20 °C until they will be QCed and sequenced.
Quantify and quality control the library using the Tapestation D1000 kits. Libraries will typically exceed 1-5 ng/μl concentration Sample on D1000 tape after 8 PCR cycles

Samples should be submitted according to your Sequencing Facility specifications. At least 8 cycles are required for the Index Read 1 ( i7 ) 28 cycles for Read 1 (BC+UMI). Read 2 (DNA) should be adjusted based on the quality of the genome being mapped to, but for human and mouse 50 cycles is sufficient. Some potential sequencing options: A B C D E F 1 Sequencer Read 1 Read 2 Index Read (i7) Index Read (i5) Kit 2 NovaSeq 28 90 10 10 NovaSeq SP v1.5 100 cycle 3 NextSeq 2000 28 52 10 10 NextSeq 2000 P3 50 cycles 4 HiSeq 28 110 10 0 HiSeq 3000/4000 150 cycles
Incubate for 00:10:00 at 80 °C
Prepare the following lysis buffer to be dispensed by the FlexDrop %s %s

Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix Store on ice until use A B C 1 Reagent Well uL 384w plate + 20% 2 UltraPure Water 0.6 265 3 Maxima RT Buffer (5x) 0.5 230.4 4 100uM Biotin RAP-TSO 0.025 11.52 5 Maxima H Minus RT 0.075 34.56 6 Total (incl lysis buffer) 2.5 1152
Add 1.3 µL Reverse Transcription Mix to each well with the FlexDrop
Incubate for 01:00:00 at 50 °C
Incubate for 00:10:00 at 80 °C
Pool all wells of each plate by inversion into a SBS reservoir using a centrifuge 400 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes collect into a 5mL LoBind tube.
Perform 1x SPRI ratio cleanup with SPRI beads diluted 1:4 in SPRI buffer
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads

Wash with 2 mL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant Volume of EtOH should be adjusted depending on the number of samples. More samples will require more EtOH to cover the beads completely.
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Daniel prefers 2 minutes to 5 minutes to reduce risk of overdrying. It is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute the beads in 45 µL of UltraPure Water
Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT off the magnet Incubate for 00:02:00 on the magnet and transfer 43 µL to a new PCR tube or plate
Prepare Pre Amplification Mix A B 1 Reagent 1x 2 KAPA HiFi 2x RM 25 µL 3 10uM RAP_PCR-1_Fwd 2 µL 4 10uM RAP-FL_Rev 2 uL 5 UltraPure Water 1 µL 6 Total 30 µL
Add 30 µL Pre Amplification Mix to 20 µL cDNA sample

Incubate the Pre Amplification PCR as follows: %s %s Adjust the number of cycles based on input (sample number, cell number, or concentration). As a general guide we recommend: Daniel's experience is it is better to err on more PCR cycles for this full length PCR and less for the 2nd library PCR. %s %s
Mix sample with 40 µL SPRIselect for a 0.8x SPRI ratio
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~5 min) and discard supernatant
Wash with 180 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute cDNA in 12 µL UltraPure Water

Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT off magnet then 00:02:00 on magnet Transfer 10 µL to a new PCR tube or plate Stopping Point. Samples can be safely stored at -20 °C and protocol can be continued at a later date.
Quantify the cDNA using Qubit DNA HS following the manufacturer's protocol. Use 2 μl of clean cDNA for quantification.

Quality check the cDNA using the Tapestation D5000 tape. Passing the cDNA quality check does not guarantee that the data will be of high quality, however, if the cDNA fails the quality check it will usually not yield good libraries and will therefore generate lower quality data.
Turn on a lab oven to 50 °C and another to 65 °C
Dispense 1.2uL lysis buffer to dried minibulk v2 ( PRM_0000 ) primer plates using the FlexDrop
Remove 384w plates from -80C freezer, thaw on ice and quick spin
Incubate 65 °C for 00:05:00 After this step increase the oven temperature to 80 °C for later use.
Remove 384w plates from -80C freezer, thaw on ice and quick spin
Incubate 65 °C for 00:05:00 After this step increase the oven to 80 °C for later use
You may store the sample plates at -80 °C at this point
Remove tubes from magnet and quick spin in centrifuge. Use a large centrifuge for 5mL tubes if necessary
Remove tubes from magnet and quick spin in centrifuge. Use a large centrifuge for 5mL tubes if necessary
Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT off the magnet Incubate for 00:02:00 on the magnet and transfer 43 µL to a new PCR tube or plate
Add 5 µL of ExoI Buffer (10x) and 2 µL µL of Exonuclease I. Incubate as follows: A B C 1 Step Temp Time 2 Digest 37 20 3 Heat inactivation 80 10 4 Storage 4 Infinite
Add 2 µL of ExoI Buffer (10x) and 1 µL of Exonuclease I. Incubate as follows: A B C 1 Step Temp Time 2 Digest 37 20 3 Heat inactivation 80 10 4 Storage 4 Infinite
Perform 0.8x SPRI bead size selection with 40 µL undiluted SPRI beads.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads
Place the tube on the magnet stand for 00:05:00 and discard supernatant
Wash with 180 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute the beads in 22 µL of UltraPure Water
Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT off magnet 00:02:00 on magnet then transfer 20 µL to a new PCR tube or plate
Perform 0.8x SPRI bead size selection with 40 µL undiluted SPRI beads.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads
Place the tube on the magnet stand for 00:05:00 and discard supernatant
Wash with 180 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant
Repeat wash step once more
Air dry beads for 00:02:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.
Elute the beads in 22 µL of UltraPure Water
Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT off magnet 00:02:00 on magnet then transfer 20 µL to a new PCR tube or plate
Measure the approximate volume in each cDNA pooled tube by measuring by pipette Should be less than 870 µL
Prepare 900 µL diluted AMPure RNA XP beads: 1:4 in SPRI buffer per tube 225 µL, RNA beads to 675 µL, SPRI buffer
Perform 1x SPRI ratio cleanup with SPRI beads diluted 1:4 in SPRI buffer
Measure the approximate volume in each cDNA pooled tube by measuring by pipette Should be less than 870 µL

The fastqs may be preprocessed with your pipeline of choice. I prefer zUMIs where the parameters are: file1: name: path_to_read1.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(19-28) - UMI(1-18) file2: name: path_to_read2.fastq.gz base_definition: - cDNA(1-90)

When I have multiple plates in a run I have the bclconvert script write the index reads to file and concatenate fastqs from all plates into a single file: cat *I1_001.fastq.gz > combined_S1_I1_001.fastq.gz cat *R1_001.fastq.gz > combined_S1_R1_001.fastq.gz cat *R2_001.fastq.gz > combined_S1_R2_001.fastq.gz

I then use the index read as the first part of the well barcode: sequence_files: file1: name: combined_S1_I1_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(1-8) file2: name: combined_S1_R1_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - BC(19-28) - UMI(1-18) file3: name: combined_S1_R2_001.fastq.gz base_definition: - cDNA(1-90)

You will need to create a well barcode whitelist by concatenating the index reads used to all well barcodes: # Read index read 1 barcodes plateBC <- read.csv(here::here( "i7_only_Kit_TT_Set_A.csv" )) # Read well barcodes wellBC <- read.csv(here::here( "barcodeOrder_v1.csv" )) # Define the vectors to be concatenated vector1 <- plateBC$i7_8nt vector2 <- wellBC$Well_BC # Get all combinations combinations <- expand.grid(vector1, vector2) # Concatenate the combinations combinations <- as.data.frame(paste(combinations$Var1, combinations$Var2, sep="")) # Write the result to a CSV file write.csv(combinations, file = here::here(" all_sample_BCs.csv "), row.names = FALSE, quote = F, col.names = FALSE) all_sample_BCs.csv is the barcode_file in the zUMIs yaml
creator: Daniel V Brown
